title: Kafka Cluster Connection
description: Enterprise only. Defines how to connect to a Kafka cluster

client_tls.title: TLS client options
client_tls.description:
Enables specific TLS connection options when connecting to the Kafke brokers. Supports all options under [TLS client settings](https://www.krakend.io/docs/service-settings/tls/#client-tls-settings).

See: https://www.krakend.io/docs/enterprise/backends/grpc/

sasl.title: SASL Connection options
sasl.description: Enables to authenticate to the Kafka service using user and password

dial_timeout.title: Dial Timeout
dial_timeout.description: Dial timeout for establishing new connections

read_timeout.title: Read Timeout
read_timeout.description: Maximum time allowed to read from broker

write_timeout.title: Write Timeout
write_timeout.description: Maximum time allowed to write to broker

keep_alive.title: Keep Alive
keep_alive.description: Time to maintain an idle connection open

client_id.title: Client ID
client_id.description: A name to give to the client stablishing the connection

rack_id.title: Rack ID
rack_id.description: A name to identify the rack we are connecting from

channel_buffer_size.title: Channel Buffer Size
channel_buffer_size.description: The number of events to buffer in internal and external channels. This permits the producer and consumer to continue processing some messages in the background while user code is working, greatly improving throughput

metadata_retry_backoff.title: Metadata Retry Backoff
metadata_retry_backoff.description: The time between attempts to reconnect when disconnected

metadata_retry_max.title: Metadata Maximum Retries
metadata_retry_max.description: When a disconnection happens, the client needs to refresh its metadata to know the current state of the kafka cluster (effectively the number of attempts to reconnect)
